methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.0B - $8.6B.

data.narrative:
Cohere, a Series D AI & Software company founded by Aidan Gomez, Ivan Zhang, and Nick Frosst, is evaluated against a comp universe of 5 private peers in the same sector and stage (Wayve at $8.6B, Hugging Face at $4.5B, ElevenLabs at $3.0B, Hive AI at $2.0B, and Labelbox at $1.0B), plus 1 public comparable.

Two valuation methods were applied to triangulate fair value. Stage-progression analysis, which applies a typical 1.5x step-up multiplier to Cohere's last post-money valuation of $7.0B, implies a forward value of $10.5B. Peer-median methodology across the 5 same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $3.0B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $8.6B, reflecting wide dispersion in the frontier AI space.

The triangulation analysis supports a valuation range of $2.5B (low) to $12.1B (high), with a median of $6.8B. Cohere's current $7.0B valuation sits near the median and aligns with the upper range of peer medians, consistent with the company's capital raised ($1.6B to date) and stage positioning. Key uncertainties include the wide peer dispersion—a 8.6x spread—reflecting heterogeneous business models and AI subsectors, and whether stage-progression premiums remain justified in a market environment where frontier AI valuations remain unsettled.
